topic: Multiplicative Goodman-Bacon for staggered Poisson difference-in-differences: characterize the probability limit of naive TWFE PPML under staggered adoption and heterogeneous proportional effects.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the population pseudo-true single-coefficient unit-and-time-FE PPML estimand under staggered adoption and unrestricted cohort-time proportional-effect heterogeneity, including exactly when it equals a causal proportional ATT.

source_refs.claim: Shows that wrong comparisons extend to TWFE PPML through a two-individual/three-period example, but nonlinear lower-level effects do not aggregate naturally.
source_refs.author: Ninon Moreau-Kastler
source_refs.venue: EU Tax Observatory Working Paper No. 31 / SSRN

source_refs.claim: Identifies cohort-calendar-time effects under an exponential conditional mean with fully flexible PPML, rather than interpreting a misspecified pooled coefficient.
source_refs.author: Jeffrey M. Wooldridge

source_refs.excerpt: TWFE coefficients decompose into own-treatment and other-treatment contamination; no dCDH/DHS equality row
This v2 deliberately withdraws any staggered-adoption or estimator-rate claim

open_problems.why_unsolved: Moreau-Kastler supplies a minimal PPML illustration, while Wooldridge avoids the pooled coefficient by estimating flexible cohort-time effects. The archived reviews identify the still-missing equality row between a causal DiD functional and a residualized projection, and the nearby nonlinear proposal withdrew staggered adoption entirely. The obstruction is concrete: in an exponential mean, the FE projection and its residualized-cell weights depend on the heterogeneous effects through the pseudo-true fitted mean, so the linear Goodman-Bacon weighted-average identity cannot be transferred by exponentiation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A misspecified-Poisson population-score theorem giving existence and uniqueness of the FE-PPML pseudo-true root beta-star, its closed form in a two-cohort/three-period design, and an implicit weighted-FWL response formula for every cohort-time log effect.

writing_exemplar.why: Its framing directly connects nonnegative outcomes, proportional effects, the canonical ratio-of-ratios, and the failure of staggered TWFE PPML.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: nonnegative outcomes motivate PPML proportional effects -> a canonical 2x2 PPML contrast targets a ratio-of-ratios -> staggered heterogeneous adoption makes the pooled PPML coefficient an unresolved nonlinear projection

method_exemplars.technique: Treat pooled PPML as a misspecified QMLE/KL projection, establish its population score root, and differentiate the first-order conditions using the information Hessian.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested coefficient is a pseudo-true score root rather than an identified common-effect parameter.

method_exemplars.technique: Use exponential-mean PPML imputation from untreated observations to define cohort-time counterfactual effects and its QMLE limit theory.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The same multiplicative parallel-trend structure separates the correct flexible target from the misspecified pooled coefficient.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a sharp effect-dependent forbidden-comparison sign condition for how a cohort-time proportional effect changes pooled unit-and-time-FE PPML, including a non-vacuous staggered two-cohort/three-period sign-reversal configuration.

source_refs.claim: Its staggered 2x3 PPML example says some treatment effects scale down the pooled estimate and can potentially reverse its sign, by analogy with linear negative weights.
source_refs.author: Ninon Moreau-Kastler
source_refs.venue: EU Tax Observatory Working Paper No. 31 / SSRN

source_refs.claim: Proves in linear TWFE that treatment-cell weights may be negative, so the coefficient can be negative although all cell effects are positive.
source_refs.author: Clément de Chaisemartin and Xavier D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.excerpt: Add a smallest nontrivial three-period/two-cohort influence-function weight table
comparison-set incidence matrices, FWL residualization, quotient by ker(a), Σ-whitening

open_problems.why_unsolved: The linear negative-weight theorem establishes the comparison pathology but not its nonlinear analogue. In PPML the relevant treatment residual is computed in mean-weighted FE geometry and changes with the pseudo-true fitted mean, which makes a fixed OLS-style signed-weight decomposition Jensen-obstructed. Moreau-Kastler reports the symptom rather than a necessary-and-sufficient local rule; the prior reviewer requests independently demand a smallest worked timing table and actual FWL geometry. Thus one numerical PPML witness cannot establish either the derivative sign or its persistence on an open regime.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An implicit-function identity d beta-star/d delta_gt = E[Wtilde_it times d mu_it/d delta_gt] / E[w-star_it Wtilde_it squared] whose sign is characterized by the pseudo-true weighted-FWL residual Wtilde_gt, plus a theorem identifying negative-Wtilde treated cells and an explicit open 2x3 positive-effect sign-reversal regime.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames the nonlinear problem as the concrete extension of the familiar wrong-comparison critique.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: linear staggered TWFE uses already-treated units as controls -> negative comparison weights can reverse signs -> PPML has the same empirical symptom but lacks an effect-dependent signed-comparison theorem

method_exemplars.technique: Residualize treatment on group and time effects to derive signed comparisons, then prove a positive-cell-effect/negative-coefficient construction.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested PPML result is the nonlinear analogue of its exact negative-weight and sign-reversal diagnosis.

method_exemplars.technique: Differentiate the misspecified population score through the pseudo-true parameter and establish denominator positivity from the information Hessian.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired sign is a comparative static of a pseudo-true QMLE root, not a fixed linear projection coefficient.

open_problems.open_problem: Establish analytical cluster-robust influence-function inference for the counterfactual-share-weighted proportional treatment effect estimated by a PPML imputation ratio-of-ratios under staggered adoption.

source_refs.claim: Defines an imputation ratio of observed to predicted counterfactual means, argues consistency as N grows, and recommends a unit-resampled panel bootstrap for standard errors.
source_refs.author: Ninon Moreau-Kastler
source_refs.venue: EU Tax Observatory Working Paper No. 31 / SSRN

source_refs.claim: Derives efficient imputation estimation, asymptotic behavior, and inference for linear staggered-adoption event-study estimands under unrestricted effect heterogeneity.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Xavier Jaravel, and Jann Spiess

source_refs.excerpt: define Gamma_m as the gap between each named estimator IF and the true efficient EIF
whether the BJS imputation weights are primitive population weights or the Sigma-optimal weights

open_problems.why_unsolved: The proportional target is a ratio whose denominator contains counterfactual shares predicted by a PPML FE first stage; that first-stage error is shared across treated cells, so cell effects cannot be treated as independent observations. Moreau-Kastler uses bootstrap standard errors and does not give an asymptotic linear representation or analytical variance. The banked reviewer feedback pinpoints the two missing ingredients: state the aggregation weights as the causal target rather than projection artifacts, and derive the estimator's actual influence function. Linear imputation theory is structurally close but does not by itself linearize this nonlinear PPML ratio.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem identifying the positive counterfactual-share-weighted proportional ATT together with a cluster-level asymptotic-linear influence-function representation for its PPML imputation ratio-of-ratios, a consistently estimable sandwich variance, and a valid normal limit.

writing_exemplar.why: It defines the exact proportional estimand and imputation estimator whose bootstrap-only inference needs completion.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: multiplicative parallel trends identify untreated counterfactual means -> their treated-sample ratio identifies a percent change of the average -> analytical inference must propagate PPML counterfactual estimation through that ratio

method_exemplars.technique: Derive an imputation estimator's asymptotic representation while accounting for the first-stage untreated-outcome fit, then construct inference from the resulting influence function.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Both procedures impute untreated outcomes in staggered panels; the required extension is the nonlinear ratio and PPML first stage.

method_exemplars.technique: Use a QMLE score expansion and sandwich covariance for the PPML nuisance fit before applying a ratio delta method.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The PTT denominator is a plug-in functional of PPML-estimated fixed-effect nuisance parameters.

method_exemplars.technique: Use canonical-link PPML and untreated-observation imputation to construct multiplicative counterfactual means and cohort-time effects.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the nonlinear DiD imputation architecture whose aggregate ratio is the target of the requested limit theory.

literature_map:
Moreau-Kastler (2025, EU Tax Observatory WP 31; SSRN 5247936) is the direct anchor: it extends wrong comparisons to TWFE PPML and proposes an imputation ratio-of-ratios target.
Moreau-Kastler gives a two-individual/three-period PPML illustration but not a general pseudo-true-weight theorem.
Its discussion explains why a linear aggregation of cell semi-elasticities targets a different object: nonlinearity/Jensen breaks proportional-effect aggregation.
It defines the counterfactual-share-weighted percent change of the average, argues consistency of its imputation ratio, and uses a unit bootstrap for standard errors.
Wooldridge (2023, Econometrics Journal, DOI 10.1093/ectj/utad016) identifies nonlinear cohort-time effects under multiplicative parallel trends by flexible canonical-link QMLE and imputation.
That flexible specification intentionally avoids interpreting the misspecified pooled coefficient.
Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics, DOI 10.1016/j.jeconom.2021.03.014) gives the canonical linear staggered-TWFE 2x2 decomposition.
de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER, DOI 10.1257/aer.20181169) prove negative linear TWFE cell weights and sign reversals despite positive effects.
Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics, DOI 10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.09.006) show heterogeneous-effect contamination of conventional linear event studies.
Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics, DOI 10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001) provide group-time ATT identification, estimation, and inference for staggered DiD.
Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, Review of Economic Studies; arXiv:2108.12419) derive efficient linear imputation event-study estimation and inference.
White (1982, Econometrica, DOI 10.2307/1912526) supplies pseudo-true QMLE and sandwich-expansion machinery for a misspecified pooled PPML score.
Silva and Tenreyro (2006, Review of Economics and Statistics, DOI 10.1162/rest.88.4.641) gives the separate PPML motivation against log-linearization with zeros and heteroskedasticity.
Nagengast and Yotov (2025, AEJ: Applied Economics) use fully interacted PPML effects in gravity; Moreau-Kastler argues their aggregation target differs from the ratio-of-ratios.
The live gaps are the exact nonlinear geometry and sign behavior of pooled PPML, and analytical inference for the positive imputation target.
prior_proposal_map:
The active panel_ppml_forbidden_comparison qid contains only initialization/state material, so it contributed no direct predecessor.
The closest nonlinear-DiD predecessor is _bank/failed/eid_binary_did_scale_frontier_v1; its §11 expressly withdrew staggered-adoption and estimator-rate claims after a finite binary frontier.
The continuous-treatment predecessor eid_continuous_did_acr_panel and its angle0_v5 review isolate the missing equality row between causal DiD functionals and residualized TWFE limits.
The eid_bjs_efficient_event_study bank entry, rejected angles, and reviews were the highest-yield adjacent source.
Its angle0_v3 strengthening path requests a smallest nontrivial three-period/two-cohort weight table.
Its angle2_v3 request specifies comparison incidence, FWL residualization, and whitening rather than an abstract row-space claim.
Its angle2_v5 reviewer request insists on an actual estimator influence function; angle1_v5 asks whether imputation weights are primitive population weights or optimal projection weights.
These are adjacent, not duplicates: they concern linear, continuous-dose, or binary-scale DiD, not FE-Poisson pseudo-true projection under proportional effects.
